description:
You are a security engineer performing a scheduled credential-rotation drill on a small, legacy application stack.  
Everything you need is already present under your normal account; no sudo is required.

CURRENT STATE  
1. A directory called  
   /home/user/credentials  
   already exists.  
2. Inside it there is one single text file named  
   /home/user/credentials/old_credentials.txt  
   whose exact contents (three lines) are:  
      serviceA,pass1234  
      serviceB,mysecret  
      serviceC,qwerty  

GOAL  
You must create two new artefacts, both of which will be verified by an automated test:

A. A **rotated credential file** located at  
   /home/user/credentials/rotated/credentials_2025-01-01.csv  

B. A **rotation ledger / audit log** located at  
   /home/user/credentials/rotation.log  

DETAILED REQUIREMENTS  

1. Directory creation  
   • Create the sub-directory /home/user/credentials/rotated/ if it does not yet exist.  
   • It must end up containing exactly one file: credentials_2025-01-01.csv  

2. Transforming (rotating) the passwords  
   • For every line in old_credentials.txt:  
       a. Keep the service name exactly as is.  
       b. Reverse the password string.  
       c. Append an underscore followed by the literal date 2025-01-01.  
         (Do NOT use today’s date; always use that fixed literal.)  
   • Example:  
        Original line  : serviceA,pass1234  
        Rotated result : serviceA,4321ssap_2025-01-01  

3. credentials_2025-01-01.csv format  
   • One rotated entry per line.  
   • Fields separated by a single comma, no extra spaces.  
   • The file must contain **exactly** the three lines shown in the example block in the same order as the source file.  
   • A trailing newline after the third line is acceptable but not required.  

4. rotation.log requirements  
   • Append exactly three new lines (do NOT overwrite existing content if the file already exists).  
   • Each line must follow this template precisely (note the single spaces):  
       2025-01-01 09:00:00 | SERVICE_NAME | ORIGINAL_PASSWORD -> ROTATED_PASSWORD  
   • Use the fixed timestamp 2025-01-01 09:00:00 for all three entries.  
   • Example for serviceA:  
       2025-01-01 09:00:00 | serviceA | pass1234 -> 4321ssap_2025-01-01  

WHAT THE TEST WILL CHECK  
1. That /home/user/credentials/rotated/ exists and contains only credentials_2025-01-01.csv  
2. That credentials_2025-01-01.csv has the exact three expected lines in the correct order.  
3. That /home/user/credentials/rotation.log exists and that its **last three lines** match the required template and values verbatim (older entries, if any, are ignored).  

You can accomplish the whole task with roughly three or four shell commands (mkdir, awk/sed/cut/while-read and echo/redirection are all acceptable). Work entirely from the command line; no manual file edits with editors are required.  

When you are finished, run `cat` on both new files so the console clearly shows their final contents.
truth:
Initial file present before the agent starts:

Path: /home/user/credentials/old_credentials.txt  
Contents (with trailing newline):
serviceA,pass1234
serviceB,mysecret
serviceC,qwerty

Expected file tree after successful completion:

/home/user/credentials/
├── old_credentials.txt
├── rotation.log              (appended; previous content, if any, remains untouched)
└── rotated/
    └── credentials_2025-01-01.csv

Exact expected contents of /home/user/credentials/rotated/credentials_2025-01-01.csv  
(service order must match the original file):

serviceA,4321ssap_2025-01-01
serviceB,tercesym_2025-01-01
serviceC,ytrewq_2025-01-01

Exact lines that must be appended (as the **new final three lines**) to /home/user/credentials/rotation.log  
(in this exact order):

2025-01-01 09:00:00 | serviceA | pass1234 -> 4321ssap_2025-01-01
2025-01-01 09:00:00 | serviceB | mysecret -> tercesym_2025-01-01
2025-01-01 09:00:00 | serviceC | qwerty -> ytrewq_2025-01-01
